name: level0-literals
description: nil, booleans, integer, float, and string literals
source: print(nil, true, false, 42, 3.5, "literal")

name: level0-locals-scope
description: local bindings, reassignment, and block shadowing
source:
local x = 1
local y = x + 1
print(y, x)
do
  local x = y + 1
  print(x)
end

name: level0-arithmetic
description: integer and float arithmetic with precedence and unary minus
source:
local a, b = 7, 2
print(a + b - 2, a - b, a * b - 2, a / b, a // b, a % b, -a)

name: level0-strings
description: string concatenation, length, escapes, and equality
source:
local s = "lua" .. "-" .. "55"
print(s, #s, "a\n" == "a\n")

name: level0-tables
description: basic table construction, get/set, overwrite, and array length
source:
local t = {1, 2, 3, name = "lua"}
t[2] = 22
t.extra = "three"
print(t[1], t[2], t.extra, #t)

name: level0-float-table-keys
expected_stdout:
half	two	quarter	raw	nil
true	31
description: non-integral float table keys work through constructors, indexing, raw operations, and pairs traversal
source:
local t = {[1.5] = "half", [2.0] = "two"}
t[3.25] = "quarter"
rawset(t, 4.5, "raw")
print(t[1.5], t[2], rawget(t, 3.25), rawget(t, 4.5), rawget(t, 4))
local u = {[1.5] = 10, [2.0] = 20, a = 1}
local saw_float = false
local sum = 0
for k, v in pairs(u) do
  if k == 1.5 then saw_float = true end
  sum = sum + v
end
print(saw_float, sum)

name: level0-control-flow
description: if, while, repeat, numeric for, break, and short-circuit expressions
source:
local sum = 0
for i = 1, 5 do
  if i % 2 == 0 then sum = sum + i end
end
local n = 0
while n < 3 do n = n + 1 end
repeat
  sum = sum + n
  break
until false
print(sum + ((true and 10) or 0))
print(n)

name: level0-comparison-boolean-bitwise
expected_stdout:
true	true	true	true	true	true
ok
2	7	5	12	3	-4
description: comparison, boolean short-circuit value results, and integer bitwise operators
source:
local a, b = 6, 3
print(a > b, a >= 6, b < a, b <= 3, a == 6, a ~= b)
print((false or "fallback") and "ok")
print(a & b, a | b, a ~ b, a << 1, a >> 1, ~b)

name: level0-if-else-false-branch-regression
description: false if conditions dispatch to the else branch
source:
local value = "initial"
if false then
  value = "then-branch"
else
  value = "else-branch"
end
print(value)

name: level0-numeric-for-loop-local-regression
description: numeric for loop variables are local to the loop body and do not mutate outer locals
source:
local i = "outer"
local total = 0
for i = 1, 3 do
  total = total + i
end
print(i, total)

name: level0-lazy-and-or-short-circuit-regression
description: and/or skip unevaluated RHS expressions that would otherwise raise
source:
local a = false and (missing + 1)
local b = true or (missing + 1)
print(a, b)

name: level0-integer-float-equality-regression
description: integer and float values compare equal when numerically equal

name: level0-token-aware-unsupported-regression
description: unsupported-feature detection ignores load/debug substrings inside identifiers and strings
source:
local preload_value = "debug words are data"
local loader_count = 9
print(preload_value, loader_count)

name: level0-advanced-api-local-shadowing-regression
description: local variables named like advanced APIs shadow global hooks and do not trigger fallback markers
source:
local rawget, rawset, rawequal, rawlen = 1, 2, 3, 4
local collectgarbage, coroutine = 5, 6
local pairs, ipairs, next = 7, 8, 9
local pcall, xpcall, error, setmetatable = 10, 11, 12, 13
print(rawget, rawset, rawequal, rawlen)
print(collectgarbage, coroutine, pairs, ipairs, next)
print(pcall, xpcall, error, setmetatable)

name: level0-advanced-api-field-name-regression
description: table field names that look like advanced APIs are not global API usage
source:
local t = { rawget = 3, setmetatable = 4 }
print(t.rawget, t.setmetatable)

name: level0-repeat-until-local-advanced-shadowing-regression
description: repeat-block locals remain in scope for the until condition and can shadow advanced API globals without fallback markers
source:
repeat
  local rawget = true
until rawget
print("repeat-shadow")

name: level1-functions
description: direct function definitions and calls with positional arguments
source:
local function add(a, b) return a + b end
local function twice(x) return add(x, x) end
print(add(twice(5), 5))

name: level1-direct-call-statement
description: direct function calls are valid statements and can update visible locals
source:
local x = 0
local function setx() x = 3 end
setx()
print(x)

name: level1-primary-call-statement-chains
description: standalone table-field, method-sugar, indexed, and returned-table call chains are valid call statements
source:
local seen = {}
local a = { b = { c = { marker = "self", f2 = function(self, k, n) seen[1], seen[2], seen[3] = self.marker, k, n end } } }
a.b.c:f2("k", 12)
print(seen[1], seen[2], seen[3])
local t = {}
t.fn = function(a, b) t[1], t[2] = a, b end
t.fn("x", "y")
print(t[1], t[2])
t[1] = function(a, b) t[2], t[3] = a, b end
t[1](4, 5)
print(t[2], t[3])
local function factory() return { run = function(a, b) print(a, b) end } end
factory().run("chain", 99)

name: level1-advanced-api-local-function-shadowing-regression
description: local function and parameter bindings named like advanced APIs do not trigger fallback markers
source:
local function pcall(rawget) return rawget + 1 end
local function setmetatable(error) return pcall(error + 1) end
print(setmetatable(5))

name: level1-varargs
description: vararg packing and assignment adjustment
source:
local function count(...)
  local t = {...}
  return #t, t[1], t[#t]
end
print(count(1, 2, 3, 4))

name: level1-empty-inner-varargs
description: an empty inner vararg function call must not inherit outer varargs
source:
local function outer(...)
  local function inner(...) return ... end
  return inner()
end
print(outer(1, 2))

name: level1-closures
description: closure captures and independent upvalue cells
source:
local function counter(start)
  local value = start
  return function()
    value = value + 1
    return value
  end
end
local a = counter(10)
local b = counter(5)
print(a(), a(), b())

name: level1-named-closure-escape
description: named nested closures that escape must execute correctly or be explicitly unsupported
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  return inner
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level1-multiple-returns
description: multiple returns in assignment and table constructor adjustment
source:
local function values() return 1, 2, 3 end
local a, b, c = values()
local d = 4
local t = {values()}
print(a, b, c, d, #t)

name: level1-tail-call-observable
description: tail-call returned values remain observable at the caller boundary
source:
local function finish(x) return "done", x end
local function bounce(x)
  if x == 0 then return finish(9) end
  return bounce(x - 1)
end
print(bounce(3))

name: level1-tail-call-missing-args
description: tail-call observable return adjustment preserves missing positional arguments as nil
source:
local function f(a, b, c) return c, b end
local function g() return f(1, 2) end
local a, b = g()
print(a, b)

name: level1-env
description: ordinary lexical _ENV lookup through an explicitly supplied environment
source:
local _ENV = { print = print, value = 21 }
print(value)

name: level1-env-function-assignment
description: unresolved assignments inside functions write through the active lexical environment
source:
local env = { print = print }
local _ENV = env
local function f() x = 7 return x end
print(f(), env.x, x)

name: level1-env-definition-time-lookup
description: functions resolve unresolved names through their definition-time lexical _ENV, not caller locals or caller _ENV
source:
local env1 = { print = print, value = 1 }
local env2 = { print = print, value = 2 }
local _ENV = env1
local function f() return value end
do
  local _ENV = env2
  print(f())
end

name: level1-env-definition-time-assignment
description: unresolved assignments inside functions write through the definition-time lexical _ENV, not the caller _ENV
source:
local env1 = { print = print }
local env2 = { print = print }
local _ENV = env1
local function f() x = 11 return x end
do
  local _ENV = env2
  print(f(), env1.x, env2.x)
end

name: level1-env-caller-local-shadow
description: caller locals must not satisfy unresolved names in functions defined under a different lexical environment
source:
local env = { print = print, value = 1 }
local _ENV = env
local function f() return value end
do
  local value = 2
  print(f())
end

name: level1-globals
description: ordinary global assignment, lookup, and cleanup
source:
corpus_global_value = 31
print(corpus_global_value)
corpus_global_value = nil

name: level1-global-function-assignment
description: unresolved assignments inside functions update the default global environment
source:
local function f() x = 7 return x end
print(f(), x)
x = nil

name: level1-aliased-closure-escape
description: aliased nested closures that escape must execute correctly or be explicitly unsupported
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  local alias = inner
  return alias
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level1-parenthesized-return-alias-closure-escape
description: multiline parenthesized return aliases of captured nested closures must be explicit unsupported, not silent nil success
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  local alias = inner
  return (
    alias
  )
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level1-parenthesized-local-alias-closure-escape
description: local aliases initialized from parenthesized captured nested closures must be explicit unsupported, not silent nil success
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  local alias = (inner)
  return alias
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level1-parenthesized-assignment-alias-closure-escape
description: assignment aliases from parenthesized captured nested closures must be explicit unsupported, not silent nil success
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  local alias
  alias = (inner)
  return alias
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level1-multiname-local-closure-escape
description: multi-name local aliasing of a captured nested closure must execute correctly or be explicitly unsupported
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  local first, second = nil, inner
  return second
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level1-multitarget-assignment-closure-escape
description: multi-target assignment aliasing of a captured nested closure must execute correctly or be explicitly unsupported
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  local first, second
  first, second = nil, inner
  return second
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level1-global-assignment-closure-escape
description: assignment of a captured nested closure to an outer/global name must execute correctly or be explicitly unsupported
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  escaped = inner
end
outer(5)
print(escaped())
escaped = nil

name: level1-table-assignment-closure-escape
description: assignment of a captured nested closure through a table field must execute correctly or be explicitly unsupported
source:
local function outer(x)
  local function inner() return x end
  local box = {}
  box.fn = inner
  return box.fn
end
local f = outer(5)
print(f())

name: level0-expected-runtime-error
description: negative deterministic fixture proving expected non-zero diagnostics are recorded
source:
local value = "not-a-number" + 1
print(value)
